about.intro: Frontend Angular Developer with 4+ years of experience building scalable enterprise web and mobile applications. Currently freelancing at Zellavora Technologies, delivering Angular and Ionic-based solutions across client projects.
bio: Previously at ITGalax Solutions Pvt Ltd, I engineered government-grade portals and hybrid mobile apps serving 10,000+ active users — improving application performance by 50% and reducing API consumption by 40%. My expertise spans government portals (Fiji Immigration Department), enterprise pension management systems (PRIMS), and cross-platform mobile apps (Vanuatu National Provident Fund).

I specialize in Angular (v13–v20), TypeScript, RxJS, Angular Signals, Ionic Angular, and Tailwind CSS. I thrive in Agile/Scrum teams, deliver reusable component-driven architecture, and maintain clean, scalable codebases for enterprise environments. Open to remote, hybrid, and full-time opportunities across India.

projects.title: Selected work — freelance, enterprise & mobile
projects.subtitle: A peek at recent products I've helped design, engineer, and ship. Each one balances scalable architecture with refined UI.

featured.alt: Galaxy Sofas — Modern Furniture Business Website
featured.name: Galaxy Sofas — Modern Furniture Business Website
featured.description: A modern, responsive business website for Galaxy Sofas, a local furniture retailer. Delivered a fresh, professional online presence with a product showcase, enquiry form, and brand identity that reflects the business's casual yet premium feel.
featured.highlights: Designed and built a fully responsive Angular website with modern UI/UX, Implemented product showcase with category filtering and smooth animations, Built a contact/enquiry form with email integration, Optimised for fast load times and SEO with structured metadata, Delivered a brand-consistent design — fresh, casual, and professional.

caseStudy.problem: Galaxy Sofas had no online presence — customers could not discover the business online, browse products, or send enquiries without calling directly. The business was losing potential leads with no digital touchpoint.
caseStudy.approach: Built a modern Angular website with a clean product showcase, enquiry form, and Google Maps integration. Focused on mobile-first design to capture walk-in traffic searching on phones, and optimised Core Web Vitals for fast organic discovery.
challenges: Translating a furniture brand's physical store vibe into a clean digital aesthetic
Ensuring fast load times for image-heavy product galleries on mobile connections
Making the site self-manageable for a non-technical business owner

grid.alt: Fiji Immigration Department — Enterprise Immigration Management System
grid.name: Fiji Immigration Department — Enterprise IMS
grid.description: A large-scale government immigration platform serving the Fiji Immigration Department with multiple role-based portals: External User, Internal Staff, Police Verification, and Payment Gateway.
grid.highlights: Engineered 4 role-based portals with JWT Auth and RBAC, Built complex multi-step forms, enterprise validation modules, and workflow systems, Developed and published shared Angular component libraries used across all portals, Integrated REST APIs and backend Web API services across the entire platform.

grid.alt: PRIMS — Pension & Retirement Information Management System
grid.name: PRIMS — Pension & Retirement Information Management System
grid.description: An enterprise pension management platform for HR teams and administrators, handling retirement information and administrative workflows. Built using Zoneless Angular for next-generation performance.
grid.highlights: Built responsive dashboards and reusable UI modules for enterprise administration, Enhanced rendering using Angular Signals and optimized change detection strategies, Reduced API consumption via RxJS caching and stream optimization.

grid.alt: VNPF — Vanuatu National Provident Fund App
grid.name: VNPF — Vanuatu National Provident Fund App
grid.description: A cross-platform hybrid mobile application for the Vanuatu National Provident Fund, delivering insurance and provident fund services to citizens on Android and iOS in the Pacific region.
grid.highlights: Built full Android & iOS app using Ionic Angular and Capacitor, Implemented biometric authentication — Face ID and Touch ID, Optimized API communication for low-bandwidth Pacific region environments.

blog.title: Angular deep-dives & engineering insights
blog.subtitle: Sharing what I learn building enterprise Angular applications — performance, architecture, and modern patterns.

articles.title: Angular Signals vs RxJS: When to Use Each in Enterprise Apps
articles.excerpt: After migrating three enterprise Angular apps from zone-based RxJS patterns to Signals-first architecture, here's what I've learned about where each shines — and where mixing them is the right call.
content: Enterprise Angular teams often ask whether Signals replace RxJS. The honest answer: they complement each other.
Signals excel at synchronous, component-local state — form toggles, derived UI flags, and template-ready values without async pipe overhead.
RxJS remains essential for event streams, HTTP orchestration, debounced search, and multi-source coordination.
In migrations I've led, we moved view-state to signals first, then wrapped existing observables with toSignal() at component boundaries.
The biggest win was clarity: templates read top-down, while services still compose async workflows with operators.

articles.title: The Angular Performance Audit Checklist I Use on Every Project
articles.excerpt: A practical, battle-tested checklist for identifying and fixing the most common Angular performance bottlenecks — from change detection to bundle size to Core Web Vitals.
content: Performance work starts with measurement, not assumptions. I run Lighthouse and Chrome DevTools on every production release candidate.
Check change detection strategy first: OnPush, signals, and trackBy on large lists eliminate unnecessary template work.
Next, audit bundle size with source-map-explorer and defer non-critical routes and animation libraries.
Images and fonts dominate LCP on marketing pages — optimize hero assets and avoid render-blocking third-party scripts.
Finally, validate INP by profiling long tasks during scroll and interaction; canvas animations and heavy observers are common culprits.

articles.title: Implementing Biometric Auth in Ionic Angular with Capacitor
articles.excerpt: A step-by-step guide to integrating Face ID and Touch ID in Ionic Angular apps using the Capacitor biometric plugin — including graceful fallback for older devices.
content: Biometric authentication improves UX for returning users, but only when implemented with secure fallbacks.
Capacitor's biometric plugin abstracts platform APIs while keeping secrets in the OS keystore.
Always offer PIN or password fallback for devices without biometrics or when enrollment fails.
Combine biometrics with refresh-token rotation and short-lived access tokens on the API layer.
Test on real devices early — emulator behavior differs significantly for Face ID and fingerprint flows.
